name: Fire Police City County
description_short: Summit Choice Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering savings accounts, personal loans, auto financing, credit cards, and home equity products with a 92-year history of community service.
description_long:
Summit Choice Credit Union has been serving its community for 92 years as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution. The organization emphasizes its local roots and commitment to member relationships, contrasting itself with larger commercial banks by highlighting its personal service approach and community involvement ($85K given back annually, 111 hours volunteered). The credit union operates a mobile app platform for account management and maintains physical branches staffed with named loan officers like Rick and Kristi Baer who are specifically mentioned in member testimonials.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of consumer financial products including savings accounts (standard, youth, and money market), certificates of deposit (CDs), share certificates, auto loans, personal loans, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), and Visa credit cards with rewards programs. Featured rates displayed on their homepage include auto loans as low as 3.99% APR (up to 72 months), certificates up to 4.00% APY, money market accounts up to 1.50% APY, and youth savings up to 2.34% APY. They also offer a DreamPoints rewards card that provides points on everyday purchases and currently advertise 0% APR on credit card purchases for 12 months.

What distinguishes Summit Choice is their explicit positioning as a community-focused alternative to "Big Banks" with emphasis on member relationships and personalized service. Multiple testimonials highlight specific staff members by name and reference long-term member loyalty (some since 1984-1985). They advertise competitive promotional rates, no closing costs on HELOCs during promotional periods, and a rewards program tied to membership activities beyond just card usage. The organization actively communicates member benefits through annual meetings and community engagement metrics.

As a credit union, Summit Choice operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance rather than FDIC, which is standard for the category. The website presents only positive information with no disclosed limitations, fees, or eligibility requirements. Member testimonials are genuine but represent self-selected positive experiences. The institution appears stable and established, though specific information about membership requirements, credit score thresholds, or service area limitations is not disclosed on the homepage.
services: Certificates of deposit and share certificates (up to 4.00% APY)
Auto loans (3.99% APR for up to 72 months promotional rate)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOCs with no closing costs promotional offer)
Visa credit cards with 0% APR promotional rate on purchases for 12 months
DreamPoints rewards credit card with purchase points
Online banking and mobile app access
Business lending services (mentioned but not detailed)
pros: 92-year operating history with established community presence
Competitive auto loan rates as low as 3.99% APR for up to 72 months
0% APR promotional offer on new credit card purchases for 12 months
No closing costs on HELOCs during promotional periods (March-April)
DreamPoints rewards program earning points on everyday account activities and card usage
Named, accessible loan officers (Rick, Kristi Baer) specifically mentioned in recent testimonials
Mobile app for account management and online account opening capability
cons: No information disclosed about membership eligibility requirements or application process
Service area limitations not specified on website (geographic membership restrictions typical for credit unions)
No fee schedule or pricing transparency visible on homepage
Credit card rewards program details vague (point value, redemption options not explained)
Limited financial transparency (no balance sheet, loan volume, or member count data provided)
best_for: Long-term community members seeking personalized loan service with named relationship officers
Auto buyers wanting competitive rates with fast approval processing
Savers seeking higher CD and money market rates than typical commercial banks
Consumers prioritizing local institution loyalty and community reinvestment values
diagnosis: Summit Choice Credit Union is best for members within their service area seeking relationship-based lending with competitive rates, particularly for auto loans and home equity products. The primary caveat is that credit union membership typically requires eligibility criteria (employment, geography, or family affiliation) that are not disclosed on the website, and prospective members should verify membership qualification before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Fire Police City County — NCUA-insured credit union in FORT WAYNE, IN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
